reason: The image depicts a scene that could potentially represent the founding of Saint-Domingue, but several elements raise concerns. The clothing of the overseers resembles 16th-century European attire (berets, doublets, long coats), which is appropriate. The large marble blocks and wrought iron gates are also plausible, although the scale of the marble blocks seems excessive for initial construction. The lack of clear tropical vegetation or dense foliage is somewhat uncharacteristic. The shirtless laborers are also not dressed correctly. The style of the wrought iron gate is slightly later than 1496. Overall, the image captures some of the essence of the event, but certain details detract from its historical accuracy.
